title: Arc Flash Study
bodyHTML:
Skip to content
  • Carelabz Germany
  • Services
  • Arc Flash Study

Arc Flash Study

Find Out What It's All About

Care Labs is an economical supplier of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for businesses of all types. Arc flash hazards, employee training, paperwork, and PPE (Personal Protection Equipment) requirements will be assessed by skilled specialists to verify that your electrical safety program is up-to-date, compliant, and comprehensive. 

Enquire

What is Involved

Care Labs is able to discover arc flash risks early on and take appropriate measures to mitigate the impact of harmonic distortions in order to ensure the safety of the equipment. ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is utilized to analyze and investigate Arc flashes. The quantity of light and heat produced by an arc fault is called an arc flash. This occurs when current flows between conductors via an air gap. 

The arc flash ionizes the air and emits a considerable amount of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). Arc flash analysis is the process of investigating and analysing the electrical installations of a facility in order to assess the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. By creating safety criteria for competent electricians working on equipment and circuits, arc flash studies encourage safe working conditions. Included also are revised electrical one-line schematics and equipment locations, and short circuit and coordination analysis. 

ARC FLASH RESEARCH GOALS:

  • Calculate the extent of the harm brought on the incident energy released during an arc flash fault.
  • Modifying protective equipment’s settings to reduce exposure to incident energy
  • Create safety regulations for arc flash hazards.
  • Adhere to the rules and laws that apply in your nation.
  • Determine the PPE type that was used on the project.

Negative Effects of Arc Flash

  • Serious burns to the skin, face, and eyes are brought on by arcs.
  • The trachea and lungs can suffer severe burns from inhaled gases and hot particles.
  • Injuries brought on by flying objects and loose parts.

Arc flash investigations are essential for lowering risks and improving the safety of electrical employees and the general security of the company. Studies on arc flash help identify electrical hazard levels and implement the right safety precautions to lower the risk of burns and accidents. Arc flash analysis is necessary for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial organizations that use electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switchboards.

Arc Flash Research Advantages

  • Increased machine and human safety
  • Lower medical and legal expenses
  • Adherence to safety-related legal requirements
  • A decrease in the number of code violations and penalties

The procedures listed below are used for arc flash analysis: 

  • Data gathering
  • Carrying out site visits
  • A system’s design
  • Analyzing arc flashes

Care Labs' Arc Flash Research Work Method

Care Labs offers top-notch services and will offer any services required by the building team, a nearby contractor, or an engineering business. 

The steps in our work process are as follows: 

  • Data gathering
  • An electrical power system’s ETAP modeling
  • Checking the short-circuit
  • A review of overcurrent protection device cooperation
  • Research on arc flashes
  • Carrying out arc flash tests
  • Maintaining a record
  • Techniques for arc flash danger reduction
  • Large single-line diagrams
  • Displaying arc flash warning signs
  • The finished document

Benefits of Employing Care Labs’ Services:

  • Create safer, better-regulated, and standard-compliant electrical systems.
  • Bus ratings and protection devices are incorporated with integrated modules for thorough short circuits, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation.
  • Improving workplace security
  • Quick and simple alternative design recommendation
  • Increased safety margins thanks to user-defined arc fault limits
  • Utilize our computer-aided analysis to automatically generate arc flash labels to save time.
  • Reduce maintenance costs and productivity losses.

Crew members are carefully placed throughout the Care Labs to ensure that our professionals are accessible in case of a routine or emergency. Care Labs has become an ISO 9001:2008-accredited business with excellent customer feedback very rapidly. In addition to Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Cologne, Frankfurt, and Stuttgart, we also provide arc flash investigation and analysis services in all other significant cities. For a free estimate on arc flash analysis and study, get in touch with us right now. 

Related Services

Arc Flash Study

An arc flash study, also known as an arc flash hazard analysis, is a process of analyzing electrical systems to identify potential hazards related to arc flash events.

Read More

Power System Study & Analysis

Power System Study & Analysis service helps you to have a safe, reliable, and efficient power system for your facility under normal and abnormal circumstances.

Read More

Power Quality Analysis

Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure it meets the required standards for safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.

Read More

Harmonic Study and Analysis

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical method for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on data.

Read More

Motor Start Analysis

Motor Start Analysis service helps you to get perspective on the best way of starting motor, and proper power system design for reducing the impact of the motor starting on the power system.

Read More

title: Harmonic Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:
Skip to content
  • Carelabz Germany
  • Services
  • Harmonic Study and Analysis

Harmonic Study and Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

Care Labs provides thorough harmonic research and analysis along with electrical installation services such as safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification. Professionals will assess your electrical safety program’s harmonic risks, training materials, paperwork, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to make sure it is up-to-date, comprehensive, and compliant. Care Labs can identify harmonics early on and take the required actions to reduce the effects of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety. 

Enquire

What is Involved

An electrical system can become inefficient due to harmonic voltages and currents. They produce ineffective heat and may deteriorate the quality of the electricity generated. The mathematical method of harmonic voltage analysis is used to forecast probable resonances and levels of harmonic distortion. A mathematical method for forecasting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on empirical data is harmonic voltage analysis. 

Harmonic frequencies are added to the electrical system by nonlinear electrical switching equipment such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers. The danger of equipment failure is significantly increased by high voltage/current harmonic levels. As a result, a timely harmonic investigation and analysis are required. Currently, there are 5% harmonic restrictions in systems employing 415 volts, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics.

Harmonic Distortion Consequences:

  • Responsible for high voltage and circulating current.
  • Device failure and destruction because of severe voltage distortion.
  • A rise in the internal energy of components, which leads to component failure and a shortened lifespan.
  • Fault-induced tripping of branch circuit breakers.
  • Measurement flaw.
  • Electrical and distribution system fires and explosions.
  • Generator failures.
  • A reduction in system power factor.
  • Installation and utility costs are costly.
  • Productivity reductions.
  • Inactivity and unnecessary driving.
  • Use of larger motors.

Harmonic issues are exceedingly rare, but when they do arise, they tend to decrease the power system’s dependability, power quality, and efficiency. Hence, harmonic investigation and analysis are important. 

  • Protects against current and voltage harmonics.
  • Limitation of frequency variance.
  • Unbalanced current and voltage.
  • Enhance power factor analysis and maintain a value close to one.
  • Regulates voltage fluctuations.

Harmonic research and analysis will provide insight into the probable source and effect of power quality issues, aiding in the avoidance of harmonic-related difficulties during the design phase and decreasing the likelihood of unintended repercussions.

Care Labs' workflow for harmonic analysis and research:

  • Highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and long medium-voltage cables on the electrical single-line diagram.
  • Determine the PCC (point of common coupling).
  • Locate system buses inside the facility that are sensitive to harmonic distortions.
  • Get harmonics information for all nonlinear loads.
  • Get vital information on voltage and current harmonics at the selected PCC, including the highest and lowest levels of short circuit faults.
  • Do a harmonic analysis of the electrical network using ETAP (electrical transient analysis program).
  • Determine the overall and discrete current and voltage wave distortion levels at the buses and PCC.
  • Sketch the spectrum of harmonic frequencies.
  • A suitable mitigation strategy is proposed and implemented if harmonic distortion levels surpass acceptable thresholds.
  • After implementing the mitigation solution, rerun the analysis.

Benefits of Care Labs' Services:

If background harmonics have previously been detected, a higher rating is used in the second stage. 

  • Stage 2 permits variable converter size based on fault level and converter type if the backdrop is less than 25%.
  • 50% of the permitted converter grade may be utilized if the backdrop is between 25% and 75%.
  • On the top backdrop, a thorough harmonic analysis is done.

To guarantee that our professionals are easily reachable in the event of a regular or urgent situation, Care Labs has staff members stationed in numerous places. Care Labs has quickly established itself as an organization with ISO 9001:2008 accreditation and a loyal clientele that has given excellent feedback. We carry out harmonic research and analysis in several key German towns, such as Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Cologne, Frankfurt, and Stuttgart. To arrange your harmonic analysis and study in Germany or to get an estimate, contact us right now!

Related Services

Arc Flash Study

An arc flash study, also known as an arc flash hazard analysis, is a process of analyzing electrical systems to identify potential hazards related to arc flash events.

Read More

Power System Study & Analysis service helps you to have a safe, reliable, and efficient power system for your facility under normal and abnormal circumstances.

Read More

Read More

Harmonic Study and Analysis

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical method for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on data.

Read More

Motor Start Analysis

Motor Start Analysis service helps you to get perspective on the best way of starting motor, and proper power system design for reducing the impact of the motor starting on the power system.

Read More

title: Motor Start Analysis
bodyHTML:
Skip to content
  • Carelabz Germany
  • Services
  • Motor Start Analysis

Motor Start Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

Care Labs offers specialized Motor start research and analysis services to businesses in Germany. Our motor start analysis service’s main goal is to assess the unusual effects of starting a big motor. Before starting an electric motor, there are a few additional factors to consider in addition to properly connecting it to the line voltage, including nuisance tripping, high nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which are signs of a bad power supply. 

Enquire

What is Involved

To gather data on recent and past motor installations, Care Labs will send competent employees to your location. Then calculations are done to anticipate future effects, and suggestions for reducing the effects are given. These calculations are done utilizing the gathered data and manufacturer input.

Motor starting techniques may be assessed using static and dynamic simulation methods:

  • To determine the best solution for the load flow, the measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled.
  • In pre-fault short circuit situations, the short-circuit approach is employed; for motor starting currents, voltage drop calculations are applied.
  • The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the impact of motor starting in a traditional transient stability technique if an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is provided.
  • Transient stability simulations can be used if detailed dynamic model information for an electric motor is not available.

Motor Start Analysis is Required in Germany:

Modern industrial systems increasingly require motors, and electric motor size is expanding as well. When a big motor connected to a line is started, it can interfere with nearby loads, electrically distant buses, and the electric motor. When starting an electric motor, the terminal voltage must be at 80% of the rated voltage or above. Since the square of the terminal voltage of a motor is directly proportional to torque, voltage difference affects the motor’s load torque. 

Running machines may approach their breaking torque and slow down or stop in the case of a significant voltage decrease. Voltage drops affect a variety of items, including lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment. Furthermore, a high beginning current decreases the terminal voltage, increasing the current drawn by all systems supplying a continuous load. Furthermore, faulty low-voltage relays are located. 

When is a Motor Start Analysis Necessary?

  • Before or during the purchase of a big motor.
  • If there is no generator available and the motor rating is greater than 30% of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating.
  • If the motor rating exceeds 10% to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating and the generator is the only source of power for the system.
  • If the system’s sole power source is the generator. A motor start study can be necessary if many motors start at once in an industrial setting.
  • The capacity limitations of smaller power supplies make voltage loss issues when starting a motor with a big capacity even more problematic. Understanding the output of systems that use small generators is made easier by looking at how motors start up.

Benefits of Motor Start Analysis

Review of the motor launch It is advantageous to employ a motor or starting method with advantageous speed-torque characteristics. Any unexpected loads must be accelerated under supervision to prevent going beyond the equipment’s torque limits. By performing a motor start study, the motor’s shaft functioning may be assessed. As a result, it helps in estimating the device’s acceptable torque restrictions. 

Studies on motors are starting to determine if an electric motor can start and accelerate as well as the machine’s voltage. Care Labs’ qualified staff perform motor start studies to completely assess your electric motor. In every major German city, including Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Cologne, Frankfurt, and Stuttgart, we provide motor start analysis services. Call us for pricing or reserve your motor to start studying right away!

Motor starting techniques may be assessed using static and dynamic simulation methods:

To determine the best solution for the load flow, the measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled.
In pre-fault short circuit situations, the short-circuit approach is employed; for motor starting currents, voltage drop calculations are applied.
The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the impact of motor starting in a traditional transient stability technique if an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is provided.
Transient stability simulations can be used if detailed dynamic model information for an electric motor is not available.
Motor Start Analysis is Required in Germany:

Modern industrial systems increasingly require motors, and electric motor size is expanding as well. When a big motor connected to a line is started, it can interfere with nearby loads, electrically distant buses, and the electric motor. When starting an electric motor, the terminal voltage must be at 80% of the rated voltage or above. Since the square of the terminal voltage of a motor is directly proportional to torque, voltage difference affects the motor’s load torque. 

Running machines may approach their breaking torque and slow down or stop in the case of a significant voltage decrease. Voltage drops affect a variety of items, including lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment. Furthermore, a high beginning current decreases the terminal voltage, increasing the current drawn by all systems supplying a continuous load. Furthermore, faulty low-voltage relays are located. 

When is a Motor Start Analysis Necessary?
Before or during the purchase of a big motor.
If there is no generator available and the motor rating is greater than 30% of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating.
If the motor rating exceeds 10% to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating and the generator is the only source of power for the system.
If the system’s sole power source is the generator. A motor start study can be necessary if many motors start at once in an industrial setting.
The capacity limitations of smaller power supplies make voltage loss issues when starting a motor with a big capacity even more problematic. Understanding the output of systems that use small generators is made easier by looking at how motors start up.
Benefits of Motor Start Analysis

Review of the motor launch It is advantageous to employ a motor or starting method with advantageous speed-torque characteristics. Any unexpected loads must be accelerated under supervision to prevent going beyond the equipment’s torque limits. By performing a motor start study, the motor’s shaft functioning may be assessed. As a result, it helps in estimating the device’s acceptable torque restrictions. 

Studies on motors are starting to determine if an electric motor can start and accelerate as well as the machine’s voltage. Care Labs’ qualified staff perform motor start studies to completely assess your electric motor. In every major German city, including Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Cologne, Frankfurt, and Stuttgart, we provide motor start analysis services. Call us for pricing or reserve your motor to start studying right away! 

Related Services

An arc flash study, also known as an arc flash hazard analysis, is a process of analyzing electrical systems to identify potential hazards related to arc flash events.

title: Power Quality Analysis
bodyHTML:
Skip to content
  • Carelabz Germany
  • Services
  • Power Quality Analysis

Power Quality Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

Care Labs evaluates the power quality for German companies. Our aim is to safeguard your appliances, electrical installations, and electrical equipment against issues brought on by unstable power supplies. 

Enquire

What is Involved

Data gathering, monitoring, and instrumentation equipment highly vulnerable to power supply changes is widespread among corporations and more individual users. The voltage fluctuation spectrum, which is a frequent feature of distributed energy, is something that extraordinarily little electrical equipment is designed to handle.

Care Labs can assist you with problems relating to equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning related to power quality. 

Analysis of Power Quality:

  • Evaluation of discrepancies between the load and the electrical system.
  • Analyzing the workplace to enhance power quality simulation techniques.
  • Predicting the effectiveness and functionality of load equipment
  • Improve the equipment’s dependability and power factor.
  • Limit your energy use.
  • To prevent malfunctions and interruptions.
  • Reducing anomalies in power quality such transients, spikes, and harmonics.
  • Assist in avoiding PLC lockups and voltage/frequency spikes.
  • To prevent, among other things, capacitor explosions, transformer overheating, and annoyance tripping.
  • Reduced utility expenses
  • Increase the durability of the electrical network and its parts.
  • Assess the variability resistance of electrical equipment.

Germany Requires a Power Quality Analysis

All electrical networks are prone to supply disruptions, which happen more frequently when power cables are damaged by storms, accidents, or other natural calamities. Power supply variations are very natural. The quality of the electricity delivered by a network is subject to three diverse types of fluctuations, disruptions, and distortions.

1. A frequency deviates from its range when it goes beyond or below its normal frequency range.

2. When the voltage form is preserved but the voltage amplitude is higher than usual, there are voltage disruptions.

2.1 Voltage spikes and decreases that last up to a minute are instances of transient fluctuations.

2.2 Under voltages and over voltages that last longer than a minute are both considered long duration voltage fluctuations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This condition develops when the load is split unevenly across the phases, leading to severe equipment heating.

2.4 Differences in voltage between neutral and earth

3. Disturbances brought on by voltage distortion, which happens when the voltage is neither sinusoidal nor homogeneous in shape.

3.1 Device heating, higher losses, inefficient power utilization, and resonance issues are all caused by harmonics and inter-harmonics.

3.2 Short-lived transients that have the potential to harm switch contact surfaces, decrease system performance, and hasten the breakdown of motor drives and control.

What Does Power Quality Analysis Contain?

It takes several ways to spot different power quality issues, including the ones listed below:  

  • Harmonic analysis: This method employs reduction techniques in addition to computer algorithms to pinpoint and anticipate potential harmonic issues.
  • Surges and transients will be found during this study.
  • This study would look at the value and trajectory of the voltage, as well as its short-term rise and fall.
  • Evaluation of the Reactive Force The preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be determined by this investigation.
  • Captive Electricity Analysis: With less fuel consumption and energy surcharges, this research would measure and synchronize captive electricity to fulfill demand.
  • Load Flow Study: Using the provided operational parameters, this analysis forecasts the amounts of power flow, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches.

Care Labs is committed to providing the best service possible to raise the caliber and reliability of your electrical installation for the benefit of all its customers. We offer power quality evaluations in all major cities, including Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Cologne, Frankfurt, and Stuttgart. Get in touch with us right now to schedule a power quality evaluation or to get a quotation!

title: Power System Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:
Skip to content
  • Carelabz Germany
  • Services
  • Power System Study and Analysis

Power System Study and Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

Electrical safety inspections, electrical system design simulations, power quality analysis studies, and power system study and analysis are just a few of the electrical installation services that Care Labs provides. ETAP, the most effective tool for power system transient analysis, is utilized by our team of electrical specialists to conduct load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. It enables us to work closely with customers to create and implement the best power compensation and solutions. 

Enquire

The Importance of a Power System Analysis

Every industrial and commercial building has to have an electrical system that is robust, dependable, and well-maintained. Even a brief loss of electricity can have disastrous effects, resulting in prolonged downtime, damage to important machinery, and costly repairs. An ongoing examination of your facility’s power system may show that it is both dependable and safe in regular and extraordinary circumstances. 

At every stage of the design, installation, and operation of a power system, thorough study is required. Our studies and assessments of power systems will use the ETAP software tool to offer an overview of the system’s performance during both normal operation and transient fault scenarios. It describes a group of original engineering analytical studies used to look at how an electric power system reacts to things that happen across different time periods. 

The condition of the electrical system at your institution is revealed by several examinations. A study report on power system analysis can include the following examples: 

  • Circuit breaker
  • Coordination of safety procedures
  • Transit of load
  • Dynamic and transient assessment
  • Arc flash examination
  • Power efficiency
  • Study of harmonics
  • Starting the engine
  • Earthing studies

It permits enterprises to set a baseline for other parts of overall plant management, including the electrical safety program. These investigations indicate the possibility of safe and reliable operations. 

Analysis of Load Flow

Through the use of load flow studies, it is possible to calculate the voltages and currents in different network segments and choose the right equipment for the job. To execute load flows, which offer data on a power system’s power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents, ETAP’s built-in load flow function may be employed. 

Short-Circuit Investigation:

To understand how fault conditions arise, a power system must conduct a short-circuit analysis. It takes a lot of effort to perform short circuit experiments manually using software like ETAP is much more effective. To investigate the impacts of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems, ETAP is used to model system protection devices. 

Quick-Term Research:

Transient studies are used to identify the transient over voltages and currents that lightning strikes and switching activities produce. The outcomes specify the necessary levels of insulation and surge protection. 

Investigations into the Coordination of Protection:

This study aims to verify the various security components on your system. This guarantees that they are synchronized and appropriate for the machinery they are guarding.

The following essential functions are typically incorporated into our power system analysis procedure: 

  • Data collecting method
  • Analysis of data and client meetings
  • Accumulating equipment data
  • Model of a single-line diagram
  • ETAP power system modeling
  • Analysis of short circuit and load flow
  • Investigations into protection coordination
  • Arc flash evaluation
  • A summary of the study report.
  • Risk assessment and mitigation planning

The following components comprise Care Labs Power System Analysis administration: 

  • Alternatives to save energy expenses and use.
  • Highlighting issue areas in reports.
  • Ideas for solutions
  • Details regarding how and where energy is consumed.
  • Verification of safety device ratings
  • It helps to prevent damage and injuries.
  • The effects of harmonics and transients

We analyze the client’s electrical system as part of our power system analysis studies, and we then offer suggestions on how to improve its efficiency and supply security. Our power system analysis and study services are available for Berlin, Hamburg, Munich, Cologne, Frankfurt, and Stuttgart. For a price or to schedule a study and analysis of your power system, get in touch with us right now! 

Related Services

An arc flash study, also known as an arc flash hazard analysis, is a process of analyzing electrical systems to identify potential hazards related to arc flash events.
